title: [Feature]: Add push-consumer delivery-contract and flow-control certification tests
problem: Push consumers introduce callback scheduling, server-initiated delivery, flow-control messages, idle heartbeats, client pending buffers, and queue-group behavior. Without focused certification tests, a push mode could regress ACK ordering, shutdown behavior, or memory bounds.
proposal: Add a dedicated test suite and operator evidence plan for push-consumer behavior. The suite should prove manual ACK ordering, no ACK on temporary failure, DLQ-before-ACK on permanent failure, bounded in-flight work, flow-control and heartbeat handling, graceful shutdown, and unchanged pull-mode behavior.
users: Maintainers reviewing push-mode readiness, release engineers validating a future push-support release, and operators needing evidence before enabling push mode in production.
delivery_semantics: Tests must prove that push mode does not ACK before durable sink success and does not treat callback completion as successful message processing.
security: Test fixtures and evidence must not include real server addresses, credentials, private subjects, payloads, or classified operational labels. Failure evidence must be sanitized before posting to GitHub issues.
acceptance: Contract tests prove ACK after durable sink success in push mode.
Contract tests prove no ACK on temporary failures in push mode.
Contract tests prove DLQ publish succeeds before original ACK in push mode.
Tests cover bounded queue overflow and graceful shutdown behavior.
Optional local NATS integration tests are marked and isolated.
tests: Add mocked unit tests, failure-injection tests, and optional NATS integration tests behind explicit markers or environment variables. Include tests for flow-control, idle heartbeat, pending limits, callback errors, and pull-mode non-regression. Run scripts/check.sh.
documentation: Update testing, operations, push-consumer evaluation, release readiness, roadmap, and changelog documentation.
closeout: Close after the release containing push-consumer certification tests is published. Include release tag, test command evidence, skipped integration-test rationale, and limitations.
